openmeter/ledger/routingrules.purpose: Pure validation layer for double-entry ledger transactions: defines the RoutingRule interface, composable Validator, and DefaultValidator encoding all permitted account-type pairings and route-field constraints. No DB access — validates []ledger.EntryInput before any persistence call.

patterns.name: RoutingRule interface + Validator composition
patterns.description: Each constraint is a named struct implementing RoutingRule{Validate(TxView) error}. Validator.ValidateEntries runs them sequentially, fail-fast on first error. New rules are appended to DefaultValidator.Rules in defaults.go — never create ad-hoc Validator instances in application code.
patterns.example:
type AllowedAccountSetsRule struct { Sets [][]ledger.AccountType }
func (r AllowedAccountSetsRule) Validate(tx TxView) error { ... }

patterns.name: TxView as the rule's only input
patterns.description: NewTxView converts []ledger.EntryInput into []EntryView (with pre-decoded Route via entry.PostingAddress().Route().Route()), exposing AccountTypes(), EntriesOf(accountType), HasAccountTypes(...). Rules must use TxView — never the raw EntryInput slice.
patterns.example:
view, err := NewTxView(entries)
for _, rule := range v.Rules { rule.Validate(view) }

patterns.name: HasAccountTypes guard before rule logic
patterns.description: Every rule that targets specific account types checks tx.HasAccountTypes(r.From, r.To) first and returns nil when those types are absent. Omitting the guard causes false positives on unrelated transactions.

patterns.name: RequireSameRouteRule for cross-account field consistency
patterns.description: Ensures entries on two different account types share the same RouteField values (currency, tax_code, features, cost_basis). Declared as Left/Right/Fields triplets in DefaultValidator. Use requireMatchingRouteFields helper internally.
patterns.example: RequireSameRouteRule{Left: ledger.AccountTypeCustomerFBO, Right: ledger.AccountTypeCustomerReceivable, Fields: []RouteField{RouteFieldCurrency, RouteFieldTaxCode}}

patterns.name: FuncRule for test-only rules
patterns.description: FuncRule wraps a plain function as a RoutingRule for test-only validation scenarios without defining a named struct. Never use FuncRule in production code.

patterns.name: ErrRoutingRuleViolated with WithAttrs for all errors
patterns.description: All rule violations return ledger.ErrRoutingRuleViolated.WithAttrs(models.Attributes{...}) with a 'reason' key and relevant context. Never return plain fmt.Errorf from a RoutingRule.

key_file_guides.role: Core types: RoutingRule interface, Validator (implements ledger.RoutingValidator), FuncRule, and all concrete rule structs (AllowedAccountSetsRule, RequireFlowDirectionRule, RequireSameRouteRule, RequireAccountAuthorizationStatusRule, RequireReceivableAuthorizationStageRule, RequireAccruedCostBasisTranslationRule, RequireFBOCostBasisTranslationRule, RequireUniqueSubAccountsRule).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All rules skip gracefully when the relevant account types are absent (HasAccountTypes check first) — this is intentional permissive-when-unrelated behaviour. Do not remove these guards.

key_file_guides.role: EntryView (wraps EntryInput + pre-decoded Route), TxView (slice of EntryView with projection methods AccountTypes/EntriesOf/HasAccountTypes/HasAccountType), and optional field comparators (optionalStringEqual, optionalDecimalEqual, etc.).
key_file_guides.watch_for: EntryView.Route() returns the decoded ledger.Route from entry.PostingAddress().Route().Route() — use this for route field comparisons, not the raw SubAccountRoute.

key_file_guides.role: DefaultValidator — the canonical production validator with all 12+ rules pre-composed in order. This is the single Validator used by historical.Ledger.CommitGroup in production.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new rule here affects every CommitGroup call in production. Test bidirectionally with TestDefaultValidator_* cases in routingrules_test.go.

key_file_guides.role: Table of acceptance/rejection scenarios for DefaultValidator. Helper addressForRoute constructs PostingAddress from route via ledger.BuildRoutingKey + ledgeraccount.NewAddressFromData.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New tests must use the addressForRoute helper and require.ErrorContains(t, err, 'ledger routing rule violated') for rejection cases.

anti_patterns: Accessing ledger.EntryInput.PostingAddress().Route().Route() directly in rule code instead of using EntryView.Route() — bypasses the pre-decoded projection.
Creating a new Validator with a subset of rules instead of extending DefaultValidator.Rules — silently removes production constraints.
Adding DB calls, external I/O, or mutable state inside RoutingRule.Validate — rules must be pure functions of TxView.
Skipping the HasAccountTypes guard in a rule — causes false positives when the rule's relevant accounts are absent from the transaction.
Returning plain fmt.Errorf from a RoutingRule instead of ledger.ErrRoutingRuleViolated.WithAttrs — breaks the error-type contract used by callers.

decisions.decision: Rules are applied sequentially and fail-fast (first error returned) rather than collecting all violations.
decisions.rationale: Routing violations are programmer errors or abuse, not user-facing validation flows that need full error accumulation. Fail-fast simplifies rule logic.

decisions.decision: TxView pre-decodes the Route from SubAccountRoute.Route() at construction time.
decisions.rationale: Avoids repeated decode calls inside each rule and makes field-comparison helpers in requireMatchingRouteFields straightforward.

decisions.decision: DefaultValidator is a package-level var, not a constructor function.
decisions.rationale: Rules are stateless structs; a single shared instance is safe and avoids repeated allocation on every CommitGroup call.

code_examples.scenario: Validate entries before booking a transaction (production usage pattern)

openmeter/ledger/testutils.purpose: Provides reusable test fixtures for ledger integration tests: Deps wires concrete account/resolver/historical-ledger adapters directly (no app/common), and IntegrationEnv spins up a real Postgres DB, runs migrations, creates a test customer, provisions all account types, and exposes typed sub-account helpers.

patterns.name: Build deps from constructors, not Wire
patterns.description: InitDeps wires accountadapter.NewRepo, accountservice.New, historicaladapter.NewRepo, resolversadapter.NewRepo, and resolvers.NewAccountResolver directly — never via app/common — to avoid import cycles. Two accountservice.New calls are intentional (see decisions).
patterns.example:
repo := accountadapter.NewRepo(db)
accountService := accountservice.New(repo, locker)
historicalLedger := historical.NewLedger(historicalRepo, accountService, accountService, routingrules.DefaultValidator)

patterns.name: IntegrationEnv as the single test harness entry point
patterns.description: All ledger integration tests call NewIntegrationEnv(t, namespacePrefix) to get a fully provisioned namespace, customer, and account set. Never replicate DB setup, migration, or account provisioning inline.

patterns.name: Use t.Context() throughout
patterns.description: Every Ent call and every service method in IntegrationEnv and Deps passes t.Context(), not context.Background(). This ties cancellation and DB connections to the test harness.

patterns.name: Freeze clock in NewIntegrationEnv
patterns.description: Time is frozen with clock.FreezeTime(now) and unfrozen with t.Cleanup(clock.UnFreeze) so temporal ledger queries (balance snapshots, period windows) are deterministic.

patterns.name: Typed sub-account helpers per account type
patterns.description: FBOSubAccount, ReceivableSubAccount, AccruedSubAccount, WashSubAccount, EarningsSubAccount, BrokerageSubAccount call GetSubAccountForRoute with the correct typed params (CustomerFBORouteParams, CustomerReceivableRouteParams, BusinessRouteParams). Never construct SubAccount directly.

patterns.name: SumBalance reads settled balance only
patterns.description: SumBalance calls HistoricalLedger.GetSubAccountBalance and returns .Settled(). Tests that need pending/unsettled components must call GetSubAccountBalance directly.
patterns.example: sum := env.SumBalance(t, fboSubAccount) // alpacadecimal.Decimal of settled balance

key_file_guides.role: Wires the three concrete ledger services (AccountService, ResolversService, HistoricalLedger) from a raw *entdb.Client and *slog.Logger. Exported Deps struct exposes all three for tests to call directly.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The single accountservice.New call (without Querier) before historicalLedger construction is load-bearing — historical.NewLedger requires an accountService to be passed as both the internal service and the Querier. Do not collapse or reorder these calls.

key_file_guides.role: Full integration harness: DB provisioning via omtestutils.InitPostgresDB, migrations via migrate.New+Up, customer row insertion via raw Ent (not customer.Service), account provisioning via ResolversService.CreateCustomerAccounts and EnsureBusinessAccounts.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Customer row is inserted directly via Ent because the test package must stay independent of app/common and customer/service to avoid import cycles. Do not switch to customer.Service calls here.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set — causes import cycles and couples test helpers to the full DI graph.
Constructing ledger.SubAccount literals directly instead of calling the typed IntegrationEnv helper methods.
Using context.Background() instead of t.Context() in test helpers — leaks connections and severs cancellation.
Skipping clock.FreezeTime in tests that depend on temporal ledger queries — produces non-deterministic balance windows.
Calling migrate.Up more than once per IntegrationEnv instance — wasteful; always reuse the single env per test.

decisions.decision: Single accountservice.New call before historicalLedger construction (no two-phase pattern).
decisions.rationale: historical.NewLedger receives accountService as both its internal service and Querier. Constructing one instance and passing it twice is correct and avoids a circular dependency.

decisions.decision: Direct Ent customer row insertion instead of customer.Service in NewIntegrationEnv.
decisions.rationale: Keeps testutils independent from app/common and customer/service packages, preventing import cycles in test-only code per the test-003 enforcement rule.

decisions.decision: Typed sub-account helper methods per account type (FBOSubAccount, ReceivableSubAccount, etc.).
decisions.rationale: Wrapping GetSubAccountForRoute in named helpers ensures routing-rule params are always correct for each account class and makes tests readable without duplicating route param construction.

code_examples.scenario: Set up and use a full ledger integration test
